title: Progressive disclosure /report — 3-tier CEO dashboard
description: The /report skill currently dumps everything at one level. Restructure into 3 tiers: (1) Headline — 5-line status (what shipped, what's blocked, what needs CEO), (2) Summary — per-goal progress and recommendations (current daily mode), (3) Deep dive — full initiative details and agent performance (current weekly mode). Headline is the default. CEO drills down with /report summary or /report deep.

in: Restructure /report SKILL.md into 3 tiers: headline (default), summary, deep
Headline tier: 5 lines max — shipped count, blocked count, needs-CEO count, top recommendation, health status
Summary tier: current daily mode output
Deep tier: current weekly mode output
CLI argument parsing: /report (headline), /report summary, /report deep, /report weekly (alias for deep)

dod.criterion: /report with no args produces a 5-line headline
Headline tier is readable and actionable in <10 seconds

tasks.title: Design 3-tier structure — what goes in each tier, argument parsing
tasks.scope: Define exact content for each tier. Headline: which 5 signals? Summary: same as daily? Deep: same as weekly? Map CLI args to tiers.
tasks.review_notes: Marcus: 3-tier design is sound — headline/summary/deep maps well to CEO attention budget. 5 signals (shipped, blocked, needs-CEO, health, top-action) compressed into 3 content lines is effective. Morgan: argument routing design is clean — shorthand aliases (h/s/d/w) plus legacy backward-compat (daily->summary, weekly->deep) covers all paths.

tasks.title: Build headline tier — extract top-level signals from existing report data
tasks.scope: Implement headline output: read same data sources as current /report, extract 5 key signals, format as 5-line output
tasks.review_notes: Marcus: headline template is CEO-readable — 3 content lines pack 5 signals with clear labels (Shipped/Blocked/Needs CEO/Health/Top action). Derivation rules are explicit and prioritized. <10 second scan is achievable. Minor concern: SKILL.md line 63 says '5-line status snapshot' but line 370 says 'exactly 3 lines of content after heading' — these are consistent (5 total = heading + blank + 3 content) but wording could confuse an executing agent. Not blocking.

tasks.title: Refactor /report SKILL.md — add argument routing, map daily→summary and weekly→deep
tasks.scope: Update /report skill to parse args (none=headline, summary, deep, weekly=deep alias), route to correct output tier
tasks.review_notes: Morgan: argument parsing at SKILL.md line 11-15 is correct — empty/headline/h -> headline, summary/s/daily/d -> summary, deep/weekly/w -> deep. Backward-compat aliases work: daily->summary and weekly->deep. Data gathering is properly tiered (headline minimal, summary adds detail, deep adds weekly analysis). Drill-down hint at line 80 and line 374 ensures discoverability. The daily->summary alias is implemented in SKILL.md but not listed in scope line 17 — scope only mentions weekly alias. Implementation is correct per task t3 title; scope was under-specified.

tasks.title: Test all three tiers and verify backward compatibility with /report daily and /report weekly
tasks.scope: Run all tier variants, verify output matches expectations, confirm old daily/weekly args still work
tasks.review_notes: Marcus+Morgan joint review: all tier variants verified against SKILL.md. Summary tier matches former daily format with sections intact (External Intelligence, Internal Health, Autopilot, Corrections Caught, What Changed, Project Inventory, Decision Queue, OKR Snapshot). Deep tier matches former weekly format with additions (Executive Summary, What Shifted, Team Performance, Corrections table, Lessons, Recommendations). Failure handling table at lines 339-356 covers 17 edge cases. No sections dropped from prior version (confirmed via diff — only renames and additions).
